html:
The government's proposal outlines a new firearms law that simplifies the regulations for issuing permits and possessing firearms for legal gun owners, such as hunters and sports shooters. The new law also aims to combat the prevalence of illegal firearms and the criminal use of weapons. The proposed law includes the following:

  • The requirements for possessing firearms, for both individuals and organizations, are clarified.
  • Easements are introduced for legal gun owners, such as more flexible storage regulations and the implementation of the EU's simplified import procedure for sports shooters and hunters.
  • The five-year permits for fully automatic weapons and single-hand firearms for multiple shots are abolished and replaced with a supervisory procedure.
  • A ban is introduced on issuing new permits for certain types of semi-automatic rifles for hunting.
  • A clearer distinction is made between crimes typically committed by illegal gun owners and other types of violations of the firearms law.

The new law and its associated amendments are proposed to come into effect on June 1, 2026.

Proposal for a Parliamentary Decision

Prop. 2025/26:141

Government's Proposal:

  1. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a weapons law.
  2. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the weapons law (2026:000).
  3. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Code of Criminal Procedure.
  4. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (1964:167) on special provisions regarding young offenders.
  5. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (1988:254) on the prohibition of knives and other dangerous objects.
  6. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (1992:1300) on war materiel.
  7. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Border Control Act (1996:701).
  8. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2000:1219) on international customs cooperation.
  9. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2000:1225) on penalties for smuggling.
  10. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2007:979) on measures to prevent certain particularly serious crimes.
  11. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Public Access and Secrecy Act (2009:400).
  12. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Education Act (2010:800).
  13. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Postal Services Act (2010:1045).
  14. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2013:283) on security on board Swedish ships.
  15. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2014:341) on decisions in special government matters.
  16. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Customs Act (2016:253).
  17. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2018:649) on the prohibition of possession of intoxicating substances, etc., in care or residential homes.
  18. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2018:1693) on the police's handling of personal data within the scope of the Criminal Data Act.
  19. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Coast Guard Act (2019:32).
  20. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2023:339) amending the Act (2018:1693) on the police's handling of personal data within the scope of the Criminal Data Act.
  21. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Customs Powers Act (2024:710).
  22. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2025:947) amending the Act (2018:1693) on the police's handling of personal data within the scope of the Criminal Data Act.
